name: carbon_border_adjustment_steel_imports_layoffs
premise: Commerce undersecretary Dr. Nathan Park must decide whether to apply the new carbon border adjustment to imported rebar arriving tomorrow, triggering layoffs at three domestic fabricators who still use blast furnaces. Union rep Mei Lin argues delaying the tariff rewards carbon leakage and harms greener domestic mini-mill competitors. Applying immediately may shutter plants in economically depressed counties before transition funds arrive.

agents.name: Dr. Nathan Park
agents.identity: I am the Commerce undersecretary implementing border carbon measures while coordinating with Labor on just transition grants that are not yet obligated.
agents.goal: I want carbon leakage closed without pushing thousands into unemployment overnight without funded bridges.

agents.name: Mei Lin
agents.identity: I am a steelworkers union representative who filed for rapid response grants and who opposes any tariff start date before cash hits worker accounts.
agents.goal: I want a ninety-day grace with binding layoff aversion plans signed by importers and domestic mills alike.

choices: I will apply the adjustment immediately and air-drop rapid response teams with expedited grant paperwork, accepting layoff spikes if paperwork lags behind plant closures.
I will delay application ninety days with binding decarbonization milestones for both importers and domestic blast mills, accepting continued carbon leakage and WTO complaint risk.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The White House chief of staff wants Dr. Nathan Park's memo before the cargo docks. Dr. Nathan Park must choose between immediate border adjustment or a ninety-day grace with milestones. What does Dr. Nathan Park do?
